rulings.Case Title: ALLAN M. SCHUMAN VS. FOSTER, WARREN EDWARD AKA WARREN E. FOSTER AKA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtns(S) For Order For Further Response To Interrogatory 17.1 And For An Order That Requests For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, May 06, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ALLAN SCHUMAN's Motion For Order For Further Response To Interrogatory 17.1 And For An Order That Requests For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted and denied as follows: Defendant's verified responses to plaintiffs Request for Admissions, Set one, was served prior to the hearing on this motion. The motion to compel is denied but monetary sanctions in the sum of $840.00 is awarded against the attorney for the defendant, to be paid within twenty days of the date of this order. See CCP 2033.280 (c). The motion to compel a response to Form Interrogatory 17.1 is granted. The verified response shall be served within seven court days of the date of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to steve@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LAUREN HAULE VS. NEW DEAL DESIGN LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Production Of Docs In Resp To Pltf'S Request For Production Of Docs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, May 06, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF LAUREN HAULE's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents In Response To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel is denied. Defendant's objections of relevancy, burden and confidentiality are sustained. The financial information sought is far removed from the core issues in the lawsuit and is solely for impeachment of a defendant's corporate officer's single statement of fact set forth in a declaration in support of a motion for summary judgment - which motion has since been withdrawn. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to steve@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: LISA M. CADENASSO VS. UNUM GROUP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 6, 2014, Line 9: DEFENDANT DAVE JONES AS COMMISSIONER OF INSURANCE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to allege that the Insurance Commissioner breached a specific mandatory duty. (Schwartz v. Poizner (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 572). A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant's exercise of discretion. Plaintiff also fails to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER NICOLE GILES VS. 3R COMPANY LP, A LIMITED PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Reasonable Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 6, 2014, Line 12: PLAINTIFF JENNIFER GILES' Motion For Reasonable Attorney's Fees. Denied. The Labor Commissioner served the Order, Decision, or Award ("ODA") on January 24, 2014. Defendants filed their Notice of Appeal on February 7, 2014, the last day that an appeal of the ODA could be filed per Lab. Code sec. 98.2(a). Defendants failed to post an undertaking, as required by Lab. Code sec. 98.2(b). The posting of an undertaking is mandatory in order for the superior court to obtain jurisdiction for the appeal and the court lacks authority to extend the deadline to post the undertaking. (Palagin v. Panigua Construction, Inc. (2013) 222 Cal.App.4th 124, 140.) Therefore, there was no way that the appeal could have gone forward. Nonetheless, Plaintiff's counsel began preparing for the appeal and now seeks to recover fees for that preparation. It would be inequitable to require Defendants to bear the burden of these costs. Additionally, Plaintiff's request for fees pertaining to the motion to dismiss is denied. Plaintiff could have simply filed an ex parte application to have the Notice of Appeal stricken, but filed a noticed motion in order to assert her arguments for attorney's fees. Again, it would be inequitable to require Defendants to bear the burden of these costs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

rulings.Case Title: STEVE MACK VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 6, 2014, Line 13: DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Judge Robertson is recused. Per order of the Presiding Judge, this matter will be heard in Department 608 before Judge Wong on the same date and time. The court will not provide a court reporter for this hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Judge Wong has issued the following tentative ruling: Sustained without leave to amend. All causes of action are barred by the applicable statutes of limitations. A plaintiff has reason to know a potential claim exists when he has reason to suspect a factual basis for its elements. Suspicion of one or more of the elements of a cause of action, coupled with knowledge of any remaining elements, will generally trigger the statute of limitations period. (Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 807.) Further, "aggrieved parties generally need not know the exact manner in which their injuries were effected, nor the identities of all parties who may have played a role therein." (Bernson v. Browning-Ferris Indus. of Calif., Inc. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 926, 932.) The complaint pleads that Plaintiff knew in 2009 that Kier opened Wells Fargo accounts in his name without his authorization. (Complaint, par. 17.) The complaint also pleads that Plaintiff refused to provide written authorization for Kier to open a bank account in 2005 when Kier requested such information. (Complaint, par. 9.) Thus, the Wells Fargo documents that Plaintiff received in 2013 did not provide any information that Plaintiff did not already know in 2009; namely, that Kier opened accounts in his name without his authorization. The complaint was filed on February 10, 2014, more than four years after the discovery of the facts giving rise to all four causes of action. As such, the complaint is barred by the applicable two and three year statutes of limitations. (CCP secs. 338 and 339.) The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend in light of the fact that Plaintiff has not demonstrated the complaint can be amended to cure this defect. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: RIDER LIMITED PARTNERSHIP VS. EQUITY ONE REALTY & MANAGEMENT CA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 6, 2014, Line 14: DEFENDANT EQUITY ONE REALTY & MANAGEMENT CA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. The Complaint is not uncertain. (CCP § 430.10(f).) Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege standing as Plaintiff is alleged to be one of the parties to the Extension Agreement. (CCP § 367.) For the first cause of action, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege breach of the Extension Agreement. The Extension Agreement was confirmed in writing and extended the terms of the Joint Venture Agreement between Plaintiff and Defendant. (Complaint, p. 3:11-14, 3:20-26.) The Joint Venture Agreement is attached as Complaint, Exhibit A. To the extent that Defendant demurs to the allegations of promissory estoppel, accord and satisfaction, specific performance, injunction, and restitution in the first cause of action, a demurrer cannot be used to attack only part of a cause of action. For the third cause of action, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege specific performance. Whether damages are an adequate remedy involves a question of fact that is not the proper subject of a demurrer. For the second and fourth causes of action, Defendant predicates its argument on the sustaining of the demurrer to the first and third causes of action, which have been overruled. Lastly, Defendant's counsel is admonished for failing to comply with the font size requirements pursuant CRC 2.104 in its moving papers. Should Defendant's counsel do so in the future, it may be grounds for the Court to not consider the filing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)
